narrative:
Andrés Cubas, a defensive midfielder for Paraguay, holds an overall rating of 7.23/10. This places him in the top 3% of defensive midfields at this tournament. His rating range is 6.89–7.58, reflecting his performance across a substantial match sample size.

Cubas demonstrates significant defensive prowess at this World Cup, averaging 4.312 tackles, 2.062 interceptions, and 2.062 clearances per 90 minutes. However, his offensive output is minimal, with expected goals per 90 minutes at 0.003 and expected assists per 90 minutes at 0.008. These figures highlight his primary role as a destroyer rather than a creator for his side.

Cubas is undeniably a crucial defensive anchor for Paraguay, shielding the backline effectively. Scouts and fans should continue to observe his consistent tackling and interception rates in upcoming games. His contribution remains vital in disrupting opposition attacks and maintaining defensive structure.
